title: Air quality data for low-emission zones and in-vehicle exposure
summary: Air quality forecasts support low-emission-zone planning and in-cabin air management features.
problem: City authorities planning low-emission zones, and manufacturers developing cabin air-quality features, both need consistent, spatially-resolved air quality data. Ground stations provide only sparse point measurements.
solution: CAMS reanalysis and forecasts provide consistent gridded NO2, PM2.5 and ozone fields across Europe. Authorities use them for low-emission-zone design and reporting, while manufacturers integrate forecasts to trigger cabin recirculation before entering polluted zones.

copernicusRole.detail: CAMS gridded pollutant fields feed low-emission-zone design and cabin-air models alongside traffic and sensor data. Copernicus provides the consistent air-quality layer within a wider analysis.
